zh.Malmesbury, Wiltshire, England: 英國維爾爾馬爾斯波利

zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Bridget de Baynton於1505年出生在英國維爾爾布洛哈姆時，她的父親Sir Edward Baynton VIII25歲，母親Elizabeth Frances Sulyard50歲。她於1535年與Sir James Stumpe of Malmesbury在英國維爾爾馬爾斯波利結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1545年在英國維爾爾馬爾斯波利享年40歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Bridget de Baynton于1505年出生在英国維爾爾布洛哈姆时，她的父亲Sir Edward Baynton VIII25岁，母亲Elizabeth Frances Sulyard50岁。她于1535年与Sir James Stumpe of Malmesbury在英国維爾爾馬爾斯波利结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1545年在英国維爾爾馬爾斯波利享年40岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Baynton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Bainton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places called Bainton (in East Yorkshire, Northamptonshire, and Oxfordshire), or from Baynton in Wiltshire. In most cases, the placename is from the genitive case of an Old English personal name (Bǣga or Bada) + tūn ‘farm, village’.

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Bridget
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of Gaelic Brighid (pronounced ‘breed’); . This was the name of an ancient Celtic goddess, which in Gaulish would have been Brigindos, meaning ‘the exalted one’. St Brigid of Kildare ( c.450– c.525 ) is one of the patron saints of Ireland. Very few facts are known about her life. She founded a religious house for women at Kildare, and is said to be have been buried at Downpatrick, where St Patrick and St Columba were also buried. Many of the stories of miracles told about St Brigid seem to be Christianized versions of pagan legends concerning the goddess. The popularity of the name was further reinforced throughout Europe, especially in Scandinavia in the form Birgit , as the name of the patron saint of Sweden ( 1304–73 ). She was a noblewoman of Irish stock who, after the death of her husband, founded an order of nuns, the Bridgettines. Later she went to Rome and attempted to introduce religious reforms there.

variants.name: Bridgid
meanings: Anglicized form of Gaelic Brighid (pronounced ‘breed’); . This was the name of an ancient Celtic goddess, which in Gaulish would have been Brigindos, meaning ‘the exalted one’. She founded a religious house for women at Kildare, and is said to be have been buried at Downpatrick, where St Patrick and St Columba were also buried. Many of the stories of miracles told about St Brigid seem to be Christianized versions of pagan legends concerning the goddess. The popularity of the name was further reinforced throughout Europe, especially in Scandinavia in the form Birgit , as the name of the patron saint of Sweden ( 1304–73 ). She was a noblewoman of Irish stock who, after the death of her husband, founded an order of nuns, the Bridgettines. Later she went to Rome and attempted to introduce religious reforms there.

meanings: Anglicized form of Gaelic Brighid (pronounced ‘breed’); . This was the name of an ancient Celtic goddess, which in Gaulish would have been Brigindos, meaning ‘the exalted one’. St Brigid of Kildare ( c.450– c.525 ) is one of the patron saints of Ireland. Very few facts are known about her life. She founded a religious house for women at Kildare, and is said to be have been buried at Downpatrick, where St Patrick and St Columba were also buried. Many of the stories of miracles told about St Brigid seem to be Christianized versions of pagan legends concerning the goddess. The popularity of the name was further reinforced throughout Europe, especially in Scandinavia in the form Birgit , as the name of the patron saint of Sweden ( 1304–73 ). She was a noblewoman of Irish stock who, after the death of her husband, founded an order of nuns, the Bridgettines. Later she went to Rome and attempted to introduce religious reforms there.

variants.name: Brigid
meanings: Traditional name meaning ‘the exalted one’. See Bridget .

variants.name: Brigit
meanings: Traditional name meaning ‘the exalted one’. See Bridget .

variants.name: Brighid
meanings: Traditional name meaning ‘the exalted one’. See Bridget .

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Bridget de Baynton was born in 1505, in Bromham, Wiltshire, England, her father, Sir Edward Baynton VIII, was 25 and her mother, Elizabeth Frances Sulyard, was 50. She married Sir James Stumpe of Malmesbury in 1535, in Malmesbury, Wiltshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in 1545, in Malmesbury, Wiltshire, England, at the age of 40.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Bridget de Baynton est née en 1505, à Bromham, Wiltshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Edward Baynton VIII, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Frances Sulyard, avait 50 ans. Elle a épousé Sir James Stumpe of Malmesbury en 1535, à Malmesbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1545, à Malmesbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 40 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Bridget de Baynton 1505 in Bromham, Wiltshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Edward Baynton VIII 25 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Frances Sulyard 50. Sie hat 1535 in Malmesbury, Wiltshire, England Sir James Stumpe of Malmesbury geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1545 im Alter von 40 Jahren in Malmesbury, Wiltshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Bridget de Baynton è nata il nel 1505 nel Bromham, Wiltshire, England, sua padre, Sir Edward Baynton VIII, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Frances Sulyard, aveva 50 anni. Lei sposato Sir James Stumpe of Malmesbury nel 1535, nel Malmesbury, Wiltshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1545, nel Malmesbury, Wiltshire, England, all'età di 40 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Bridget de Bayntonが1505年にイギリスウィルトシュアイアブロムハムで生まれた時，父Sir Edward Baynton VIIIは25歳，母Elizabeth Frances Sulyardは50歳であった。1535年にイギリスウィルトシュアイアマルメスブリーでSir James Stumpe of Malmesburyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1545年にイギリスウィルトシュアイアマルメスブリーで40歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Bridget de Baynton(이)가 1505년 영국 윌츠히르 브롬함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Edward Baynton VIII(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Frances Sulyard(은)는 50세였다. 그녀는 1535년 영국 윌츠히르 맬메스버이에서 Sir James Stumpe of Malmesbury(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1545년 영국 윌츠히르 맬메스버이에서 40세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Bridget de Baynton nasceu em 1505, em Bromham, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Edward Baynton VIII, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Frances Sulyard, tinha 50 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir James Stumpe of Malmesbury em 1535, em Malmesbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1545, em Malmesbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, com 40 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Bridget de Baynton родилась в 1505 г. в местности бромхам, уилцхир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Edward Baynton VIII было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Frances Sulyard, 50. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir James Stumpe of Malmesbury в 1535 г. в местности малмесбури, уилцхир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1545 г. в местности малмесбури, уилцхир, Англия в возрасте 40 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Bridget de Baynton nació en 1505, en Bromham, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Edward Baynton VIII, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Frances Sulyard, tenía 50 años. Se casó con Sir James Stumpe of Malmesbury en 1535, en Malmesbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió en 1545, en Malmesbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 40 años.
